params.description: On Oct 31, 1793, David made this portrait to remember Marat, the leader of the Jacobins. The picture's backstory is about Charlotte Corday, who was against tyranny, sneaked into Marat's bathroom and assassinated the unsuspecting politician. Marat was the leader of Jacobin during the French Revolution. He was hated by many due to his cruel and brutal treatment of his political enemies. As he had a serious skin disease, he needed to soak in medicine for a long time, which gave others the chance to …
